response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced, polyglot senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship credentials, but their profile diverges significantly from this role's core requirements. Their C/C++ experience is limited to one early-career position (NI LabWindows/CVI) and a thesis project, with the bulk of recent work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang for web and geo-data platforms. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial automation domain exposure, all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Deep systems experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) transferable to performance-critical work
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership align with the mentorship expectations
Event-driven architecture experience via an open-source geo-data platform and microservices handling millions of requests per second
concerns: Modern hands-on C++/STL experience is minimal and dated; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No demonstrated industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, or secure-engineering-standards experience
No IoT protocol exposure (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) or automation/manufacturing domain background

key_factors.factor: Strong distributed-systems and performance engineering fundamentals
